sections.paragraph:
to move to and fro, Isa 28:7. Not found in the cognate languages, but it is of the same stock as wanken, with a sibilant prefixed fchwanken (ant. wagen); with a diminut. vacillo, wackeln. Comp. פּוּךְ.

Hiphil, id., Jer 10:4, וְלֹא יָפִיק “and it moveth not.” So it is commonly taken. But perhaps it ought rather to be taken causatively, “he causes it not to move.”

Derivatives פּוּקָה, פִּיק.

II.

פוק

to go out, i.q. Chald. נְפַק.

Hiphil-

(1) to give out to furnish, Psa 144:13 Isa 58:10.

(2) to cause to come forth from any one, i.e. to get or obtain from him, Pro 3:13 12:2 8:35 18:22 . LXX. λαμβάνω.

(3) to bring to an end, to let succeed, Psa 140:9.
